Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mild hyperchloremia with low CO₂, indicating possible metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for nephrotic syndrome or diabetic ketoacidosis, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend urgent clinical correlation and further nephrology and metabolic evaluation.
